design_philosophy.core_loop: Select gadgets before each level, then execute combo-based combat against waves of enemies using move sequences and button combinations
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing limited gadget selections and learning precise input sequences while maintaining momentum through combat encounters
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as Batman or Robin using movie-inspired combat gadgets and signature moves against Gotham's criminals
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Commits heavily to Mortal Kombat II engine and digitized sprites aesthetic; enforces gadget pre-selection strategy rather than dynamic switching; includes optional co-op with PvP variant where heroes can damage each other

difficulty.lives_system: Character health bar system with continues system limitations
difficulty.continues_system: Notably restrictive - reviewers cited 'lack of continues' as a frustrating design choice
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression with time limits in specific stages (third stage mentioned as having time limit)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited - no difficulty selection mentioned; gadget selection before stages provides minor strategic control but combat execution demands are fixed

progression.upgrade_system: Gadget selection system: three standard gadgets per character plus two gadgets chosen from available list; four hidden 'blueprint' gadgets unlockable
progression.unlock_mechanism: Hidden blueprint gadgets unlock through discovery; level select cheat code (LULLABY) available for accessing stages directly
replayability_hooks: Training mode allows play as Batman, Robin, or any enemy encountered
Co-op modes with both friendly and PvP variants

world_building_techniques: Digitized sprite presentation creates movie-to-game visual continuity
Stage progression implies narrative flow tied to film events
Enemy variety suggests different Gotham criminal archetypes

faction_indicators: Batman vs Robin character choice creates allegiance signal
Co-op mode variants (friendly vs PvP) indicate cooperation philosophy
danger_signals: Restrictive continues system signals old-school arcade difficulty expectations
Time limits in specific stages create pressure-based gameplay moments
environmental_storytelling: Digitized sprites grounded in film aesthetic create visual narrative authority
Stage progression structure implies movement through Gotham locations

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Batman Forever (SNES) article
